rank.reasoning: A relatable evergreen SRE/DevOps topic, but the framing is generic and well-trodden, limiting standout shareability among technical readers.

write.post: On-call doesn't burn people out because of the pages. It burns them out because you can't plan a dinner, a run, or a full night's sleep for a week straight — the tax is on the 167 hours you weren't paged, not the one you were. Fix the alert quality first: if more than half your pages aren't actionable, you don't have an on-call problem, you have a monitoring problem you're paying for in people.

output.post: On-call doesn't burn people out because of the pages. It burns them out because you can't plan a dinner, a run, or a full night's sleep for a week straight — the tax is on the 167 hours you weren't paged, not the one you were. Fix the alert quality first: if more than half your pages aren't actionable, you don't have an on-call problem, you have a monitoring problem you're paying for in people.
